results.title: Claude Code Advanced Patterns: Subagents, MCP, and Scaling to Real Codebases
results.text:
### Claude Code Advanced Patterns: Subagents, MCP, and Scaling to Real Codebases

#### March 24, 2026

A recording of this session will be distributed via email within 24 hours

Questions can be submitted at any time using the Q&A tab in the webinar portal

##### Housekeeping

Give us feedback! Rate this webinar by completing our survey that will be shared near the end of the webinar

1. Control Claude’s behavior with CLAUDE.md and 
Hooks 
2. Parallelize Claude for major productivity gains 
3. Embed Claude Code across your SDLC, from 
feature research to CI/CD 
4. Form a mental model on when tool creation is 
worth the cycles 
5. Imagine what an advanced Claude Code 
implementation could look like for your 
organization 

##### Learning Outcomes

## Customizing Claude

##### CLAUDE.md

Similar to a README, but for Claude Forced README file to give Claude instructions on project structure, common commands, personal styling tips, etc

##### CLAUDE.md for large codebases

Monorepo/CLAUDE.md

● System overview 
● Modernization Strategy 
● Team Structure 

/packages/CLAUDE.md

● Component Architecture 
● Technical Debt 
● Migration Plans 

/packages/ui /CLAUDE.md

● UI Patterns 
● Component Library 
● Test Strategy 

packages/api-client /CLAUDE.md

● API Design 
● Data Modules 
● Service Architecture 

packages/test-cases /CLAUDE.md

● Test Coverage 
● Test Patterns 
● Automation Strategy 

Hierarchical and updatable instruction management across repositories and users

Claude walks up your directory tree to discover CLAUDE.md files

CLAUDE.md Best Practices

.claude/rules/

Organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths by providing a “paths” field in the frontmatter.

claudeMdExcludes setting

Exclude CLAUDE.md files irrelevant to your project to prevent contradictory instructions.

*Managed policy CLAUDE.md files cannot be excluded.

Keep files < 200 lines

Longer files consume more context and can negatively affect instruction adherence.

How should you structure CLAUDE.md files in large codebases?

##### Hooks

/hooks Customize and extend Claude Code behavior by registering shell commands at various points in Claude’s lifecycle.

Example Usage

● Notifications: Customize how you get notified. 
● Automatic formatting 
● Logging 
● Correcting Claude behavior 

##### Customize with MCP

A standard protocol for giving Claude access to external systems: databases, ticket trackers, browsers, internal APIs Add a server Using mcp add 

Example Usage

● Analyze monitoring data: “Check Sentry and Statsig to understand the usage of the feature described in ENG-4521.” 

Ideal Use Case: Claude needs to reason over external state, such as fetching Figma designs or creating tickets, without relying on copy + paste.

When to use which feature – CLAUDE.md, Hooks, and MCP

Tools CLAUDE.md Hooks MCP

Used when…

Project-related context & instructions to prevent repeating instructions.

e.g. “use pnpm, not npm. Run tests with pytest. Follow PEP8.”

Access to external tools, databases, and APIs through a standardized protocol. e.g. query database, fetch from GitHub, send Slack messages, access Google Drive.

Deterministic automation that must always run at specific lifecycle events.

e.g. auto-format on save, run tests after edits, send notifications on completion.

## Parallelizing Claude

Parallel Claude How does it work

● Run multiple Claude Code instances simultaneously, each in its own terminal 
● Each instance works on a separate task independently with its own context 

Git worktrees

● Create isolated working directories from the same repo: claude –-worktree 
● Each Claude instance operates on a different worktree, avoiding file conflict 

##### Subagents

my-project/ 
├── CLAUDE.md 
├── README.md 
├── agents/ 
│ ├── code-reviewer.md 
│ ├── researcher.md 
│ └── writer.md 
├── package.json 
└── src/

Ideal Use Case: Parallel experimentation or investigation of the codebase, only requiring a lighter amount of information to be returned to the main agent

Well-defined roles 
When you can give the subagent a clear and relatively 
specialized role with a defined tool access levels, and 
criteria for success or completion 
Hands-off delegation 
When inspecting or interacting with the subagent’s work 
is not a priority for you and only need a task to complete 
and return a few conclusions 
Enhance context management 
Subagents can be a great strategy to manage your 
context, under the right conditions

##### Agent Teams

Orchestrate teams of Claude Code Sessions

Spin up multiple agents that communicate, coordinate, and divide-and-conquer parallelized work, now in research preview. Agent teams shine when a task can be broken into independent workstreams, so each agent can own a slice without blocking others.

##### Claude Code in GitHub Actions - powered by the Claude Agent SDK

● Run Claude Code in GitHub Actions

Getting started ● For admins: Enable Code Review in your Claude Code settings, install the GitHub App, and opt in repositories.

.claude/rules/

Organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths by providing a “paths” field in the frontmatter.

claudeMdExcludes setting

Exclude CLAUDE.md files irrelevant to your project to prevent contradictory instructions.

*Managed policy CLAUDE.md files cannot be excluded.

Keep files < 200 lines

Longer files consume more context and can negatively affect instruction adherence.

# Claude Code: Foundations

New to Claude Code? You’re in the right place.

Join Anthropic's Claude Code specialist team for a hands-on introduction to Claude Code—the agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal and your repo. In one hour, we'll go from a cold install to a fixed bug, teach Claude the rules of your codebase, and show how teams scale a single agent into a fleet. Live demos throughout, run against a real service.

## Featuring

No items found.

## What you’ll learn

- What Claude Code is and how to hand it your first task: installing the CLI, pointing it at a real repo, running the agentic loop (read, plan, act, observe) on a live bug, and using plan mode to review the strategy before it executes.
- Teach Claude Code how your codebase works: claude.md as the project briefing, /init and /clear to manage context, and skills, plugins, and sub-agents that encode your team's standards and best practices.
- Scale from one agent to a fleet: connect to your existing systems via MCP (Jira, GitHub, internal databases), choose the right model for the job (Opus for planning, Sonnet for daily execution), and move from solo one-off tasks to parallel agent teams as your needs grow.
